decks.notes: This UR Aggro deck focuses on fast, aggressive gameplay with a combination of efficient early creatures like Quantum Riddler and Thundertrap Trainer, backed up by burn spells such as Torch the Tower and Burst Lightning. The deck also includes control elements like Long River's Pull and Ill-Timed Explosion to disrupt opponents while maintaining pressure. Boomerang Basics and Splash Portal provide card advantage, while Stormchaser's Talent boosts the power of spells. The addition of Ral, Crackling Wit adds a powerful finisher to close out games quickly. This deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a mix of creatures, burn spells, and control elements.

This deck focuses on controlling the board with a combination of powerful creatures and removal spells. Aang, Swift Savior and Shiko, Paragon of the Way provide protection and evasion, while Ashling, Rekindled and Gwen Stacy offer a strong offense. The Legend of Kuruk can provide card advantage, while Get Lost and Lightning Helix deal with opposing threats. Inevitable Defeat and Day of Judgment can wipe the board if necessary, while Stock Up ensures a steady stream of cards. Seam Rip can disrupt the opponent's strategy, and the versatile mana base allows for flexibility in casting spells.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with a swarm of powerful creatures. By utilizing cards like Badgermole Cub, Llanowar Elves, and Sazh's Chocobo, the deck aims to establish a strong board presence early on. Additionally, cards like Mightform Harmonizer and Earthbender Ascension can further strengthen the creatures on the battlefield. The inclusion of cards like Escape Tunnel and Snakeskin Veil provides protection and evasion for the creatures, allowing them to deal damage efficiently. Overall, the deck focuses on aggressive gameplay and quick victories.

This Mono Green Aggro deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with efficient creatures like Badgermole Cub and Sazh's Chocobo, supported by mana acceleration from Llanowar Elves. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with creature aggression and pump spells like Archdruid's Charm and Mightform Harmonizer. Earthbender Ascension and Sapling Nursery provide additional value and card advantage. The deck also includes some protection with Snakeskin Veil and Escape Tunnel for evasion. Overall, the strategy revolves around fast, consistent damage output and maintaining pressure on the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful dragon creatures and aggressive spells. By utilizing cards such as Clarion Conqueror, Magmatic Hellkite, and Nova Hellkite, alongside support from Sarkhan, Dragon Ascendant and Voice of Victory, the deck aims to quickly bring out high-powered dragons to attack and dominate the board. Additional spells like Burst Lightning and Sear provide removal options, while the land base ensures consistent mana production to support the dragon-centric strategy. With a focus on fast, aggressive gameplay, this deck aims to swiftly defeat opponents in head-to-head combat.

The deck is focused on using a combination of control spells and cards that benefit from casting multiple spells in a turn. By using cards like Abandon Attachments and Accumulate Wisdom to control the board and generate card advantage, players can set up for big plays with cards like Combustion Technique and Firebending Lesson. Monument to Endurance helps to stabilize against aggressive decks, while cards like Boomerang Basics and Artist's Talent provide additional utility and ways to generate value. Agna Qel'a and Stormchaser's Talent act as finishers, helping to close out the game once the opponent has been sufficiently controlled.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize aggressive red creatures with efficient burn spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent and control the board. By overwhelming the opponent with fast creatures and direct damage spells, the deck aims to win the game as quickly as possible before the opponent can stabilize. The inclusion of cards like Burnout Bashtronaut and Nova Hellkite, along with a mix of burn spells, ensures a constant stream of pressure and damage output, ultimately leading to victory through sheer aggression and speed.

This UW Control deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing cards like Consult the Star Charts and Get Lost to disrupt the opponent's strategy. It aims to maintain board control with cards like Day of Judgment and Aang's Iceberg, while also utilizing cards like Seam Rip to deal with threats. Elspeth, Storm Slayer and Ajani, Outland Chaperone provide additional ways to control the battlefield. The deck's mana base includes cards like Floodfarm Verge and Meticulous Archive to ensure consistency in casting spells. The goal is to outlast the opponent and steadily build towards a win condition.

The deck focuses on aggressive gameplay using a combination of creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. Key cards include Ashling, Rekindled for damage, Flamebraider for board control, and Deceit for disruption. The deck also includes additional cards for card draw and mana fixing. The mana base consists of a variety of lands to ensure consistency in casting spells. Overall, the deck aims to establish an early presence on the board and maintain pressure throughout the game to secure a fast victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures with disruptive abilities to control the board and pressure the opponent. By combining evasive threats like Spyglass Siren and Floodpits Drowner with powerful control elements like Deceit and Bitter Triumph, the deck aims to maintain early pressure while disrupting the opponent's gameplan. The inclusion of versatile spells like Shoot the Sheriff and Essence Scatter allow the deck to adapt to different matchups, while powerful finishers like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares can quickly close out games. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of aggression and disruption.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize various control spells and abilities to disrupt and outmaneuver the opponent while building up resources and card advantage. By using cards like Gran-Gran, Accumulate Wisdom, and Firebending Lesson to control the board and draw cards, the player aims to gradually wear down the opponent's resources and defenses. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Combustion Technique and Artist's Talent to close out the game once the opponent has been sufficiently weakened. It focuses on creating a favorable board state and card advantage to secure victory.

The main concept of this Jeskai Control deck is to control the game by using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to outvalue the opponent. By managing resources efficiently and disrupting the opponent's game plan, the deck aims to stabilize the board and then win through powerful threats like Shiko, Paragon of the Way and Marang River Regent. The deck also utilizes lands that can provide additional card advantage or flexibility in mana fixing. Ultimately, the goal is to maintain control of the game until victory can be secured.

The main idea behind this deck is to aggressively attack and pressure the opponent using a combination of low-cost creatures like Bloodghast and Flamewake Phoenix, alongside support cards like Bloodthorn Flail and Monument to Endurance. The inclusion of cards like Inti, Seneschal of the Sun and Moonshadow provide additional utility and synergy within the deck. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast-paced, aggressive strategy while maintaining board presence and control through various means such as Fear of Missing Out and Tersa Lightshatter.

This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive Simic creature strategy. By utilizing efficient creatures like Badgermole Cub and Gene Pollinator, combined with mana acceleration from cards like Llanowar Elves and Quantum Riddler, the deck aims to quickly build a threatening board presence. Other cards like Spider Manifestation and Ouroboroid provide additional threats and resiliency. The inclusion of cards like Nature's Rhythm and Keen-Eyed Curator help to maintain card advantage and tempo. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Craterhoof Behemoth to close out games quickly.

The deck focuses on using graveyard recursion and powerful combo pieces to overwhelm the opponent. By utilizing cards that allow for quick reanimation and manipulation of the graveyard, such as Deceit and Superior Spider-Man, the deck can quickly set up powerful combos and out-value the opponent. The inclusion of control elements like Deadly Cover-Up and Insatiable Avarice helps to protect the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The mana base is also designed to support the deck's strategy, with a mix of swamps and dual lands to ensure consistency in casting spells.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures like Superior Spider-Man and Harvester of Misery, and then use cards like Deceit and Requiting Hex to bring them back onto the battlefield for a powerful combo. The deck also includes cards like Doomsday Excruciator and Bitter Triumph to control the board and Stock Up to draw more cards. With a mix of reanimation spells, removal, and card draw, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a powerful late-game presence.
